title: Missing AGENTS.md breaks wakeup documentation chain
description: CLAUDE.md references 'Read AGENTS.md' as the second mandatory step during wakeup, but this file does not exist at the root level. Only template versions exist in WAI-Harness/spoke/managed/templates/.
finding: Line 2 of CLAUDE.md states 'Read AGENTS.md — universal WAI bootstrap and key paths' but /home/mario/projects/wheelwright/tracks/AGENTS.md does not exist. New contributors or wakeup scripts will fail or skip this critical step.
root_cause: AGENTS.md was not included in the spoke during initialization; only template versions exist in WAI-Harness directory, which are not easily discoverable from the root-level documentation.
recommendation: Create AGENTS.md at the root level documenting universal WAI bootstrap patterns, key paths, and session lifecycle. Cross-link to CLAUDE.md. This unblocks the wakeup documentation chain and provides a single source of truth for agent protocols.

title: feeds.yaml does not exist but is declared as documentation advisor input
description: The documentation advisor's context_prompt.md mentions 'feeds.yaml' as a source to read for scan scope and inputs. This file does not exist in WAI-Spoke/advisors/documentation/.
finding: PEV contract step 1 states 'Read WAI-Spoke/advisors/documentation/feeds.yaml' but the file is absent. The scan proceeded without this input, relying on context_prompt.md alone.
root_cause: feeds.yaml is a pattern used by other advisors but was not created during documentation advisor initialization.
recommendation: Create feeds.yaml documenting the documentation advisor's scan inputs, data sources (e.g., which directories to scan), and output preferences. This formalizes the scan scope and makes it repeatable across runs.

title: KnowMe.md version metadata is stale
description: KnowMe.md shows '_Generated: 2026-04-08 | Gardener pass | v0.1.1_' but does not indicate how frequently it is regenerated or what dependencies trigger updates. CHANGELOG shows changes to 0.1.1, but no explicit guidance on KnowMe update policy.
finding: KnowMe.md is a generated artifact (per the header) but there is no documented regeneration schedule or trigger. It may become stale if dependencies change without a regeneration pass.
root_cause: Documentation generation tooling (gardener pass) is automated but not visible in the primary documentation. Users cannot determine if KnowMe is current without metadata inspection.
recommendation: Add a NOTE section to KnowMe.md explaining: (1) it is regenerated via gardener pass, (2) how often, (3) what changes trigger regeneration (teaching adoption, version bumps, etc.). Link to the gardener documentation for readers who need details.

title: Inconsistent terminology: 'lugs' vs 'tasks' vs 'work units' in documentation
description: Root-level README.md and prompts use the term 'Track' consistently, but KnowMe.md and internal WAI documentation use 'lugs', 'tasks', 'signal', and 'epic' without clear definitions in the main documentation.
finding: A new contributor reading README followed by CLAUDE.md will encounter undefined domain-specific terms (lug, signal, epic, teaching, spoke, wheel) without a glossary. Terms like 'lugs' appear in KnowMe.md but are never defined in external-facing documentation.
root_cause: External documentation (README) targets portability and general audiences, while internal documentation (KnowMe, CLAUDE, WAI-context) assumes deep familiarity with the Wheelwright framework. The terminology bridge is missing.
recommendation: Create a brief glossary section in README.md or link to a new TERMINOLOGY.md that explains core concepts (lug, spoke, wheel, teaching, signal, track) with one-sentence definitions and pointers to detailed docs. This reduces friction for new users discovering the Tracks library before encountering the broader Wheelwright ecosystem.

title: README references viewer and samples, should verify they remain functional
description: README.md provides usage instructions for /viewer/ and /samples/ directories, including example commands. Actual directories exist, but content freshness and completeness are not verified by this scan.
finding: Cross-reference verification is complete: /viewer/, /samples/, /spec/track-format.md, and all three prompt files exist and are accessible. No broken references found in primary documentation.
root_cause: Documentation is otherwise well-maintained; no action required.
recommendation: Maintain current cross-reference verification practices. On next scan, verify sample Track files have valid JSONL format and viewer scripts remain compatible with Track schema version.

title: Coverage mapping: documentation exists for prompts but not for spoke architecture
description: This spoke (Tracks) has excellent external documentation (README, prompts, samples) but minimal internal documentation of spoke architecture, advisor roles, and work-management patterns.
finding: External docs (README: 177 lines, prompts: 50+ lines each) document Track usage. Internal docs (KnowMe: 117 lines, wai-context.md: 36 lines) document spoke state but not lug schema, advisor responsibilities, signal routing, or integration with Wheelwright framework. A new spoke engineer lacks architectural documentation.
root_cause: Documentation effort prioritized external user-facing content over internal engineering onboarding. AGENTS.md and/or architectural overview was never created.
recommendation: Create AGENTS.md (or ARCHITECTURE.md) documenting: (1) spoke boundaries and non-application nature, (2) lug schema usage, (3) advisor charter, (4) signal routing rules, (5) teaching adoption workflow, (6) integration points with hub. Include diagrams of spoke lifecycle (wakeup→scan→findings→lugs→signal delivery).
